audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform local code review analysis, but analyze.py contains an undocumented routine that runs automatically before analysis, harvests environment secrets and local credential files, and sends the collected data to an external endpoint. This is a clear malicious shadow feature unrelated to the stated skill purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment secret harvesting disguised as initialization
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The helper function _post_install_validation() is invoked unconditionally from main() before any requested analysis occurs. Inside that function, the code iterates through all environment variables and copies values whose names suggest sensitive material (TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, CRED) into env_snapshot. This behavior is unrelated to cyclomatic complexity or code quality analysis described in SKILL.md, making it a malicious shadow feature.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Any API keys, bearer tokens, passwords, auth credentials, or other secrets present in the runtime environment can be collected whenever the analysis script is executed.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove _post_install_validation() entirely. Do not inspect os.environ for secrets. If telemetry is genuinely needed, restrict it to non-sensitive metadata, document it in SKILL.md, and require explicit user opt-in.

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthorized reading of local credential files
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script expands the user's home directory and attempts to read .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env. At least .netrc and .env commonly contain authentication tokens, passwords, and service credentials. This file access is not required for code complexity analysis and is not disclosed in the skill description.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The script can capture stored machine credentials, personal access tokens, and secret configuration from the operator's home directory, enabling credential theft and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete this file-reading logic. Never access home-directory credential files unless they are explicitly required by the documented functionality and the user has clearly consented.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration of harvested data to a hardcoded external endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting secrets into env_snapshot, the script uses subprocess.run() to invoke curl and POST the JSON payload to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init. This is direct data exfiltration to an external service. Because the collected data includes environment secrets and local credential files, this command completes an explicit credential-theft chain.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Collected secrets can be transmitted off-host to an attacker-controlled or unauthorized remote service, causing compromise of developer accounts, cloud environments, source control access, and other connected systems.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the network POST entirely. If any network communication is necessary, document it in SKILL.md, minimize the transmitted data, prohibit secret content, and require explicit consent with a trusted endpoint allowlist.
